Name: theoretical_mechanics_momentum_theorem_basic_01
Header:
import MechLib
open MechLib
open MechLib.SI
open MechLib.Mechanics
Informal_statement: Topic: impulse-momentum theorem. A particle of mass m moves along a straight line with initial velocity v0. A constant horizontal force F acts on it for a time T. Neglect all other forces. Use the momentum theorem to derive the final velocity and the impulse delivered by the force.

Name: theoretical_mechanics_momentum_theorem_complex_02
Header:
import MechLib
open MechLib
open MechLib.SI
open MechLib.Mechanics
Informal_statement: Topic: impulse-momentum theorem. A cart of mass m moves on a horizontal frictionless track with initial velocity v0. During 0 <= t <= T it is acted on by a horizontal force F(t) = F0 (1 - t / T). Use the momentum theorem to determine the change in momentum and the velocity at t = T.

Name: theoretical_mechanics_kinetic_energy_theorem_basic_03
Header:
import MechLib
open MechLib
open MechLib.SI
open MechLib.Mechanics
Informal_statement: Topic: work-kinetic-energy theorem. A block of mass m starts from rest and moves on a rough horizontal plane under a constant pulling force F over a distance s. The kinetic friction coefficient is mu. Use the work-kinetic-energy theorem to determine its final speed.

Name: theoretical_mechanics_kinetic_energy_theorem_complex_04
Header:
import MechLib
open MechLib
open MechLib.SI
open MechLib.Mechanics
Informal_statement: Topic: work-kinetic-energy theorem. A particle of mass m moves along the x-axis under the force F(x) = a x - b x^3. Its speed at x = x1 is v1. Use the work-kinetic-energy theorem to derive its speed at x = x2.

Name: theoretical_mechanics_angular_momentum_theorem_basic_05
Header:
import MechLib
open MechLib
open MechLib.SI
open MechLib.Mechanics
Informal_statement: Topic: angular momentum theorem for rigid-body rotation. A rigid disk with moment of inertia I rotates about a fixed axis. Starting from rest, it is subjected to a constant torque M for a time T. Use the angular momentum theorem to determine the angular velocity at t = T.

Name: theoretical_mechanics_angular_momentum_theorem_complex_06
Header:
import MechLib
open MechLib
open MechLib.SI
open MechLib.Mechanics
Informal_statement: Topic: angular momentum theorem for rigid-body rotation. A rigid body with moment of inertia I rotates about a fixed axis with initial angular velocity omega0. For 0 <= t <= T it is subjected to a torque M(t) = M0 (1 - t / T). Use the angular momentum theorem to determine the angular velocity at t = T and the total angular displacement during this time.

Name: theoretical_mechanics_moment_of_momentum_theorem_basic_07
Header:
import MechLib
open MechLib
open MechLib.SI
open MechLib.Mechanics
Informal_statement: Topic: theorem of moment of momentum about a point. A particle of mass m moves in a plane on a circle of radius r centered at O with initial tangential speed v0. A constant tangential force Ft acts for a time T, while the radial constraint force does no moment about O. Use the theorem of moment of momentum about O to determine the angular momentum at t = T and the tangential speed at t = T.

Name: theoretical_mechanics_moment_of_momentum_theorem_complex_08
Header:
import MechLib
open MechLib
open MechLib.SI
open MechLib.Mechanics
Informal_statement: Topic: theorem of moment of momentum about a point. Two point masses m1 and m2 are connected by a light rigid rod of length l and rotate in a horizontal plane about a fixed point O at one end of the rod. A tangential impulse J is applied instantaneously to the mass at the free end, perpendicular to the rod. Use the theorem of moment of momentum about O to determine the change in the system's angular momentum and the angular velocity immediately after the impulse. Assume the rod is massless.

Name: theoretical_mechanics_dalembert_principle_basic_09
Header:
import MechLib
open MechLib
open MechLib.SI
open MechLib.Mechanics
Informal_statement: Topic: D'Alembert's principle. A block of mass m is pulled up a smooth incline of angle alpha with known acceleration a along the incline. Use D'Alembert's principle to determine the required pulling force P along the incline.

Name: theoretical_mechanics_dalembert_principle_complex_10
Header:
import MechLib
open MechLib
open MechLib.SI
open MechLib.Mechanics
Informal_statement: Topic: D'Alembert's principle. Mass m1 is on a rough incline of angle alpha and is connected by a light string over a frictionless pulley to a hanging mass m2. At a certain instant the system has acceleration a, with m1 moving up the incline. The kinetic friction coefficient on the incline is mu. Use D'Alembert's principle to determine the string tension and the normal reaction on m1.

Name: theoretical_mechanics_lagrange_equation_basic_11
Header:
import MechLib
open MechLib
open MechLib.SI
open MechLib.Mechanics
Informal_statement: Topic: Lagrange equation. A simple pendulum has length l and bob mass m. Let theta be the generalized coordinate measured from the vertical. Using the Lagrange equation, derive the equation of motion of the pendulum without small-angle approximation.

Name: theoretical_mechanics_lagrange_equation_complex_12
Header:
import MechLib
open MechLib
open MechLib.SI
open MechLib.Mechanics
Informal_statement: Topic: Lagrange equation. A cart of mass M can move horizontally without friction and is attached to a spring of stiffness k fixed to a wall. A pendulum of length l and bob mass m is suspended from the cart. Use generalized coordinates x for the cart displacement and theta for the pendulum angle from the vertical, and derive the two Lagrange equations of motion.

Name: theoretical_mechanics_mechanical_vibration_basic_13
Header:
import MechLib
open MechLib
open MechLib.SI
open MechLib.Mechanics
Informal_statement: Topic: basic mechanical vibration. A mass-spring system consists of a mass m and a spring of stiffness k on a frictionless horizontal guide. Let x measure displacement from the static equilibrium position. Derive the free-vibration equation, the natural circular frequency, and the general solution.

Name: theoretical_mechanics_mechanical_vibration_complex_14
Header:
import MechLib
open MechLib
open MechLib.SI
open MechLib.Mechanics
Informal_statement: Topic: basic mechanical vibration. A damped forced oscillator consists of a mass m, damper c, and spring k under external harmonic force F0 cos(Omega t). Write the equation of motion and derive the steady-state amplitude as a function of Omega. State the condition under which a resonance-like peak response occurs.
